meta_title: For Founders: idea validation to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Founders: Build a Portfolio via Idea Validation (Tutorial)
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about a lack of portfolio. Learn how to use idea validation, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build revenue-generating SaaS products quickly.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a myth; founders don't need a resume, they need revenue. By treating your first MVP as a rapid idea validation experiment, you create a tangible asset. Use Antigravity for scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative execution to ship a functional, market-tested product in under 48 hours.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the silent killer of ambition. You’ve got the vision, but your lack of a past portfolio makes you feel like an impostor in a room full of established players. This isn't a design problem; it's a validation crisis. The market doesn't care about your resume—it cares about your ability to solve a painful, expensive problem.

We are going to bridge the gap between 'idea' and 'asset' using an aggressive, lean methodology. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid architecture and Claude Code for autonomous feature implementation, you will stop building for your portfolio and start building for your bottom line. Let's turn your lack of experience into your biggest advantage: speed.

steps.title: Define the High-Stakes Pain Point
steps.description: Identify a specific, expensive problem for a niche audience. If they aren't paying for a solution now, it's not a business.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Generate a lean database schema and API structure for a micro-SaaS that solves [Insert Pain Point] for [Insert Niche]. Prioritize Stripe integration and authentication.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project with the schema provided by Antigravity and setup Next.js auth boilerplate.'

steps.title: Rapid MVP Scaffolding
steps.description: Don't write boilerplate. Use Antigravity to generate the core infrastructure so you can focus on the unique value proposition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create the core landing page components and user dashboard shell. Include a responsive layout and a 'Join Waitlist' form that connects to a Supabase database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the generated components and ensure the database connection is secure and functional.'

steps.title: Automated Feature Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to iterate on core features. Focus on the single feature that solves the primary pain point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a logic flow for [Core Feature] that processes user input and returns a high-value result. Map the state management for this feature.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the core logic flow using the provided state management architecture. Add error handling for edge cases.'

steps.title: Validation-Driven Payment Integration
steps.description: Add Stripe immediately. Nothing validates an idea faster than a credit card transaction.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Stripe checkout session handler and a webhook listener that updates the user subscription status in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate Stripe checkout API and configure the webhook listener for subscription status updates.'

steps.title: Deployment and Market Feedback
steps.description: Ship to production immediately. Use the live URL as your 'portfolio' piece to attract early adopters.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase for security vulnerabilities and optimize for Vercel deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Prepare the project for production deployment and verify all environment variables are correctly set.'

pro_tips: Never build a landing page without a conversion tracking pixel; validation is data-driven, not intuition-based.
Ship the 'ugly' version first. If people don't buy the ugly version, they won't buy the pretty one either.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests for your payment logic; trust in your code is non-negotiable when handling money.
Focus on one 'hero' feature. If you can't explain the value in one sentence, your product is too complex.
Treat every bug report as a feature request. Listen to your first 10 users more than your own roadmap.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, spend 80% of your time on distribution. Reach out to your target audience on LinkedIn or niche communities and present your product as a solution, not a project.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to stop dreaming and start shipping. Every day you wait is a day your competitors gain ground. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
